_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize FL state-legislature batch: judson-sapp (HD-20 R freshman, FRTL-endorsed, pro-2A), ed-hooper (SD-21 R RETIRING 2026-11-03, abortion-exceptions deviation), yvonne-hayes-hinson (HD-21 D RETIRING, Equality-FL-PAC, 0), chad-johnson (HD-22 R freshman, FOP-endorsed), danny-burgess (SD-23 R, SB7026 red-flag YEA DEVIATION, not up til 2028).

profile.background: Freshman R seated Nov 2024 (FL House HD-20: Putnam + parts of Clay/Marion/St. Johns). Railroad-contracting CEO, Eagle Scout. Record limited to the 2025 session.

judson-sapp@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: freshman seated Nov 2024, so NO votes on 2018-2024 marquee bills (treated as no-evidence, not positions). Florida Right to Life 2024 endorsement (HARD). Voted YES on HB759/2025 repealing the post-Parkland age-21 long-gun restriction (House 78-34; 'a matter of consistency') — pro-2A, NOT a red-flag deviation. Sponsored HB1437/2025 charter-conversion expansion (school choice). No cross-party deviation found; record thin but clean. Church/denomination not documented.

0.note: Florida Right to Life 2024 endorsement; campaign 'Protect the Unborn' — pro-life.

1.note: No abortion-abolition advocacy documented; not in office for FL ban votes.

2.note: No position found on embryonic research/IVF.

3.note: No position found on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: No abortion-industry funding evidence either way.

0.note: Sponsored HB1437/2025 charter-conversion expansion (school choice).

1.note: No record on parental medical/gender consent.

0.note: Permitless carry already law before tenure; no individual vote.

1.note: Voted YES on HB759/2025 repealing the post-Parkland age-21 long-gun restriction (House 78-34).

profile.background: Veteran R senator (FL House 2006-14, FL Senate since 2018), Chair Senate Appropriations. 24-yr career firefighter. Freedom Index cumulative 73%.

ed-hooper@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CURRENCY FIX: NOT seeking reelection — resignation letter filed, effective 2026-11-03 (Pasco Sheriff Chris Nocco the expected GOP successor in SD-21). Solid-but-not-perfect R: YES on HB5/2022 (15-week) and SB300/2023 (6-week) abortion bans (Senate 26-13), YES on HB543/2023 permitless carry, YES on SB214/2023 2A financial-privacy. Self-stated (iVoterGuide): one-man/one-woman marriage, opposes gender-identity protections, strong school choice, religious-conscience protection, 'physically secure the southern border.' DEVIATION on the abolition cell: he publicly supports rape/incest/life-of-mother abortion exceptions (restrictionist, NOT abolitionist) -> scored FALSE there. NOT a Parkland deviation: he was OUT of office in March 2018 (left House 2014, entered Senate Nov 2018) so cast no SB7026 vote. Lifelong Baptist; 24-yr firefighter (firefighters'-union endorsement, not a police endorsement).

0.note: Voted for FL abortion bans but states rape/incest/life exceptions; no personhood-from-conception affirmation.

1.note: States abortion permissible for rape/incest/life-of-mother — supports restrictions WITH exceptions, the contrary of abolition.

0.note: Self-stated: marriage is 'a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman.'

1.note: No civil-union/domestic-partnership position found.

2.note: Self-stated: strongly opposes adding gender identity to non-discrimination protections.

0.note: Self-stated: strongly supports school choice incl. vouchers, charters, private, homeschool.

1.note: No parental-consent vote isolated.

0.note: No specific public-profession bill/vote.

1.note: Self-stated: opposes requiring individuals/businesses to provide services violating moral/religious beliefs.

0.note: Self-stated: strongly agrees the US should do more to physically secure the southern border.

1.note: No mandatory-deportation position found.

0.note: Voted YES on HB543/2023 permitless (constitutional) carry.

1.note: Self-reports 'current restrictions are sufficient'; no red-flag/AWB vote isolated.

profile.background: Progressive D, FL House HD-21 (Gainesville/Alachua), former Gainesville city commissioner and elementary-school principal. JBS Freedom Index lifetime 14%; progressive People First report card A (94-96%).

yvonne-hayes-hinson@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CURRENCY FIX: NOT running in 2026 — announced retirement ~2025-09-02 (age/family), endorsed Malik Moore to succeed her in HD-21. Uniformly progressive record: voted NO on SB300/2023 6-week abortion ban with pro-choice floor quotes; voted AGAINST HB1557/2022 'Parental Rights in Education' and a trans-youth sports ban; Equality Florida PAC-endorsed; voted NO on HB1/2023 school choice; opposed election-security bills and attempted a sit-in against the 2022 redistricting maps; spoke against the 2021 anti-riot bill (HB1); against bills denying local IDs to undocumented migrants; gun-control posture. No conservative deviation found in any of the ten areas. Church/denomination not documented.

0.note: Voted NO on SB300 6-week ban; pro-choice floor quote ('women losing autonomy to their own bodies').

1.note: Opposed abortion restrictions; pro-choice — contrary to abolition.

0.note: Equality Florida PAC-endorsed; supports LGBTQ equality — contrary to one-man/one-woman position.

1.note: No civil-union vote isolated.

2.note: Voted against a trans-youth sports ban; Equality FL-endorsed — affirms transgender ideology.

4.note: Voted AGAINST HB1557 'Parental Rights in Education' — supports LGBTQ content in schools.

0.note: Voted NO on HB1/2023 universal school choice.

1.note: No parental-consent vote isolated.

2.note: Voted against 'book bans' / academic-freedom limits — opposes CRT/SOGI restrictions.

0.note: No paper-ballot position found.

1.note: Voted against voting-restriction bills (incl. voter-ID/security measures).

3.note: Opposed mail-in/drop-box restrictions — supports expanded mail voting.

0.note: Opposed the 2021 anti-riot bill; no evidence she seeks to defund police.

1.note: Backs 'criminal-justice reform' (soft-on-crime direction); opposed anti-riot law.

0.note: No border-barrier position found.

1.note: No deportation vote isolated.

2.note: Voted against bills denying local IDs to undocumented migrants — pro-immigrant, contrary to anti-sanctuary position.

0.note: Opposed permitless-carry-direction and age-rollback bills.

1.note: Gun-control posture across FI-scored 2A votes.

profile.background: Freshman R seated Jan 2025 (FL House HD-22: Levy/Gilchrist/part of Alachua). Auctioneer / ag-sales, former Levy County Commissioner. Thin record focused on agriculture + public safety.

chad-johnson@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06: freshman seated Jan 2025 with a thin ag/public-safety record and no culture-war sponsorships, so scored mainly from his own cited campaign statements + a hard FOP endorsement. STRONGEST: Fraternal Order of Police endorsement (HARD) plus sponsored pro-LE legislation that became law (CS/HB847 DNA-testing-grant, Ch. 2025-141). Self-stated 'pro-life, pro-family, pro-gun,' 'fight for school choice and parental rights'; House bio lists religion 'Christian' and NRA membership. No cross-party deviation found. Most issue cells null — vague slogans are not positions.

0.note: Campaign Issues page: 'I will always fight for school choice and parental rights.'

1.note: 'Parental rights' slogan too vague to map to medical/gender consent.

0.note: Fraternal Order of Police endorsement; sponsored CS/HB847 DNA-testing-grant (became law, Ch. 2025-141) + a penalty-enhancement bill.

1.note: No cashless-bail/rogue-prosecutor statement.

0.note: Self-stated 'pro-gun, Pro-Second Amendment Rights'; House bio lists NRA membership.

1.note: No red-flag/AWB position; no DEVIATION found.

profile.background: R senator, FL Senate SD-23 (Hillsborough/Pasco); former FL House member, ex-Zephyrhills mayor, ex-Dir. FL Dept. of Veterans' Affairs, Army Reserve JAG major. Chairs Pre-K-12 Appropriations. Freedom Index 83%. SD-23 is odd-numbered — not up until 2028.

danny-burgess@FL.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CURRENCY: SD-23 is odd-numbered and NOT up in 2026 (last elected 2024, term through 2028) — sitting senator, not a 2026 candidate. Deep socially-conservative record: YES on HB5/2022 (15-week, floor quote 'life begins at conception... killing babies') and SB300/2023 (6-week); YES on SB254/2023 gender-care ban for minors; floor-defended HB1557/2022; YES on permitless carry HB543/2023; YES on SB1718/2023 immigration crackdown (E-Verify + anti-sanctuary); PRIME Senate sponsor of the 2021 anti-riot law (HB1, 'have law enforcement's back'). Practicing Roman Catholic; FFPC 'Champion of the Family' (2016). DEVIATION (highest-value catch): as a FL House member he voted YEA on the 2018 post-Parkland SB7026 (red-flag risk-protection orders + age-21 long-gun + bump-stock ban) — official roll 'Y Burgess-38' — scored FALSE on the red-flag cell. HB1/2023 school-choice individual vote unverified (JBS labels it 'NO' on fiscal grounds) -> nulled, not assumed.

0.note: Voted YES on HB5/2022; floor: 'I believe that life begins at conception... we are killing babies.'

1.note: Voted for FL bans (restrictions); no abolition advocacy documented.

0.note: Practicing Catholic; no explicit one-man/one-woman statement isolated.

1.note: No civil-union vote isolated.

2.note: Voted YES on SB254/2023 banning gender-affirming care for minors.

4.note: Floor-defended HB1557/2022 'Parental Rights in Education' (Don't Say Gay).

0.note: Prime Senate sponsor of the 2021 anti-riot law (HB1); 'the least we could do to have [law enforcement]'s back.'

1.note: No cashless-bail/rogue-prosecutor vote isolated.

0.note: State legislator; no border-barrier/military vote.

1.note: No mandatory-deportation vote isolated.

2.note: Voted YES on SB1718/2023, which includes anti-sanctuary provisions.

3.note: Voted YES on SB1718/2023 mandating E-Verify for employers.

0.note: Voted YES on HB543/2023 permitless (constitutional) carry.

1.note: DEVIATION: as a House member voted YEA on 2018 SB7026 creating red-flag risk-protection orders + age-21 long-gun + bump-stock ban (roll 'Y Burgess-38').
